overview: When Claude or Codex CLI running inside a web-terminal session reaches a stage that requires user attention (waiting for input, confirmation prompt, task completion, or error), show a bell icon notification in the toolbar. Users can click the bell to see a notification list, then click a notification to jump to the relevant session. This prevents users from having to stare at terminals waiting for AI tools to finish.
goals: Detect when Claude Code CLI or Codex CLI reaches an actionable state (idle prompt, confirmation, completion, error)
Display a bell icon with unread count badge in the toolbar
Provide a dropdown notification panel listing recent notifications with session context
Clicking a notification switches to that session; if not already on that session, also opens the sidebar to show session list
Support browser Notification API when page is not in foreground
Support optional notification sound with a toggle button to enable/disable
nonGoals: No custom notification sound files — use system default notification sound only
No persistent storage of notifications across page reloads
No notification settings page or per-session notification config
No detection for non-AI tool sessions (plain terminal sessions do not trigger notifications)
successMetrics: Notifications appear within 2 seconds of AI tool reaching an actionable state
Zero duplicate notifications within 5-second debounce window for the same session
Bell badge count accurately reflects unread notification count
Clicking a notification from a different session correctly switches session and opens sidebar
Clicking a notification from the current session does not open sidebar, just focuses terminal
Browser notification fires when document.hidden is true
Sound toggle persists across the session via localStorage
openQuestions: Exact ANSI-stripped output patterns for Codex CLI completion and error states may need refinement after testing
Should notifications auto-dismiss after a certain time or only on manual clear?

stack.framework: Vanilla JS (no framework, matches existing codebase)

routes.name: Main App
routes.purpose: Single-page app — notification bell added to existing toolbar

uiNotes: Bell icon positioned in toolbar-actions area, before existing buttons
Unread badge: small red circle with count number, top-right of bell icon
Dropdown panel: max-height 400px, scrollable, positioned below bell button
Each notification item shows: session name (with aiTool icon), message summary, relative time
Unread items have a subtle highlight; read items are dimmed
Sound toggle button: small speaker icon inside the notification panel header
Panel has a 'Clear all' button at the bottom
Notification panel should respect current color theme
rules: Debounce: same session cannot trigger another notification within 5 seconds
Max 50 notifications stored in memory; oldest are dropped when limit exceeded
Claude detection patterns: idle prompt '>' at line start, '[Y/n]', 'Do you want to proceed', 'waiting for your', output idle for >3 seconds after activity
Codex detection patterns: '[a/r/d]', 'approve', 'reject', output idle for >3 seconds after activity
Completion detection: AI tool process exits (onExit event)
Error detection: error keywords in output ('Error:', 'fatal:', 'panic:', 'FAILED')
Server broadcasts notification via control message { type: 'notification', sessionId, sessionName, aiTool, message } to all lobby and session WebSocket clients
Browser Notification API: request permission on first bell click; fire native notification only when document.hidden === true
Sound: use system default via new Audio or Web Audio API; toggled via localStorage key 'notifSound' (default: enabled)
Clicking notification for different session: switchSession() + toggleSidebar(true)
Clicking notification for current session: just terminal.focus(), no sidebar toggle
qualityGates: e2e test: notification appears when AI tool pattern is detected
UI test: bell icon renders, click opens panel, click notification switches session

stories.title: Server-side AI output pattern detection
stories.description: As a developer, I want the server to detect when a Claude or Codex session reaches an actionable state so that the server can broadcast a notification.
acceptanceCriteria: In server.js ptyProcess.onData callback, for sessions with aiTool set to 'claude' or 'codex', scan recent output for known patterns
Claude patterns detected: '>' prompt at line start after output, '[Y/n]' or '[y/N]' confirmation prompts, 'Do you want to proceed'
Codex patterns detected: '[a/r/d]' approval prompt, 'approve' / 'reject' keywords in prompt context
Error patterns detected for both: lines containing 'Error:', 'fatal:', 'panic:', 'FAILED'
Idle detection: if aiTool session has had output activity then no output for 3+ seconds, treat as potential idle (use a timer reset on each onData)
Debounce: do not emit notification if same session emitted one within last 5 seconds
Example: Claude session outputs '[Y/n]' -> server generates notification { type: 'notification', sessionId, sessionName, aiTool: 'claude', message: 'Waiting for confirmation [Y/n]' }
Example: Codex session outputs approval prompt -> notification with message 'Waiting for approval [a/r/d]'
Negative case: plain terminal session (aiTool is null) outputs '[Y/n]' -> no notification generated
Negative case: same Claude session triggers pattern twice within 5 seconds -> only first notification sent

stories.title: Server broadcasts notification to all WebSocket clients
stories.description: As a developer, I want notification messages broadcast to all connected clients so that every open browser tab receives the alert.
acceptanceCriteria: New function broadcastNotification(sessionId, sessionName, aiTool, message) sends control message to all lobby clients and all session clients
Control message format: { type: 'notification', sessionId, sessionName, aiTool, message, time: Date.now() }
Broadcast uses existing sendCtrl helper and CTRL_PREFIX protocol
Example: notification broadcast -> all connected WebSocket clients receive the control message
Negative case: no WebSocket clients connected -> broadcast completes without error
Process exit notification: when aiTool session's ptyProcess fires onExit, broadcast a notification with message 'Session ended (exit code: N)'

stories.title: Frontend notification state management
stories.description: As a developer, I want a client-side notification store so that the UI can track, display, and manage notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Add notifications array in app.js state: each item { id, sessionId, sessionName, aiTool, message, time, read }
addNotification(data) pushes to array, assigns unique id, caps at 50 items (removes oldest)
markAsRead(id) sets read=true for given notification
markAllAsRead() sets all to read
clearAll() empties the array
getUnreadCount() returns count of items where read===false
Both lobbyWs.onmessage and termWs.onmessage handle msg.type==='notification' by calling addNotification
Example: receive notification message -> notifications array length increases by 1, unread count increases by 1
Negative case: receive notification for a session that was already deleted -> still stored (notification is historical)

stories.title: Bell icon with unread badge in toolbar
stories.description: As a user, I want to see a bell icon in the toolbar with an unread count badge so I know when AI tools need my attention.
acceptanceCriteria: Add bell button element in index.html toolbar-actions div, before existing buttons
Bell uses Unicode character or SVG bell icon
Red badge circle with unread count number positioned top-right of bell icon
Badge is hidden when unread count is 0
Badge updates reactively whenever notifications array changes (call updateBellBadge after addNotification, markAsRead, clearAll)
Style bell and badge in style.css, respecting theme CSS variables
Example: 3 unread notifications -> badge shows '3'
Example: all read -> badge hidden
Negative case: 0 notifications -> no badge, bell still visible

stories.title: Notification dropdown panel
stories.description: As a user, I want to click the bell to see a dropdown list of recent notifications so I can review what needs attention.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking bell toggles a dropdown panel below the bell button
Panel has header with title 'Notifications' and a sound toggle button (speaker icon)
Panel lists notifications in reverse chronological order (newest first)
Each item shows: aiTool icon (robot for Claude, lightning for Codex), session name, message summary, relative time (e.g., '2m ago')
Unread items have highlighted background; read items are dimmed
Panel footer has 'Clear all' button and 'Mark all read' button
Panel max-height 400px with overflow-y scroll
Clicking outside the panel closes it
Panel respects current color theme (uses CSS variables --bg, --text-dim, etc.)
Example: 5 notifications, 3 unread -> panel shows 5 items, 3 highlighted
Negative case: 0 notifications -> panel shows 'No notifications' placeholder text

stories.title: Click notification to navigate to session
stories.description: As a user, I want to click a notification to jump to the relevant session so I can respond to the AI tool's prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a notification item marks it as read and closes the dropdown panel
If notification.sessionId !== activeSessionId: call switchSession(sessionId) AND toggleSidebar(true) to show session list with the target session highlighted
If notification.sessionId === activeSessionId: do NOT open sidebar, just call terminal.focus()
If the session no longer exists (was closed): show a brief toast 'Session no longer exists' and do nothing else
After navigation, update bell badge count
Example: user on session A, clicks notification for session B -> switches to B, sidebar opens showing B as active
Example: user on session B, clicks notification for session B -> sidebar stays closed, terminal gets focus
Negative case: notification references deleted session -> toast message, no crash

stories.title: Browser Notification API integration
stories.description: As a user, I want to receive browser notifications when the page is in the background so I don't miss AI tool prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: On first bell button click, request Notification.requestPermission() if not already granted
When addNotification is called and document.hidden === true and permission === 'granted': fire new Notification with title '[aiTool] - sessionName' and body = message
Clicking the browser notification focuses the window/tab (window.focus())
Do not fire browser notification if document.hidden === false (user is on the page)
Example: page in background, Claude session needs confirmation -> system notification popup appears
Negative case: user denied notification permission -> no browser notification, in-app bell still works
Negative case: page in foreground -> no browser notification fired

stories.title: Notification sound with toggle
stories.description: As a user, I want an optional notification sound so I can hear when AI tools need attention, with the ability to mute it.
acceptanceCriteria: When a notification arrives and sound is enabled: play system default notification sound
Use Web Audio API to generate a short sine-wave beep as fallback if system sound unavailable
Sound toggle button in notification panel header: speaker icon, toggles between enabled/disabled
Sound preference stored in localStorage key 'notifSound' (values: 'on' / 'off', default 'on')
Toggle button visual state reflects current setting (e.g., speaker vs muted speaker icon)
Sound only plays for new notifications, not when viewing existing ones
Example: sound on, new notification arrives -> beep plays
Example: sound off, new notification arrives -> no sound, bell badge still updates
Negative case: browser blocks autoplay -> gracefully catch error, no crash

stories.title: Notification styles matching theme system
stories.description: As a user, I want the notification UI to match my selected color theme so the interface feels cohesive.
acceptanceCriteria: All notification UI elements (bell, badge, dropdown panel, items) use existing CSS variables (--bg, --text-dim, --accent, etc.)
Badge uses a red/accent color that is visible across all 8 color themes
Dropdown panel background matches terminal background with slight contrast
Unread highlight color works across all themes (test Tokyo Night, Dracula, Solarized, Nord at minimum)
Notification panel adapts to mobile viewport (full width on screens <= 768px)
Example: switch from Tokyo Night to Solarized -> notification panel colors update immediately
Negative case: no theme set (default Tokyo Night) -> notification UI renders correctly

stories.title: End-to-end and UI testing
stories.description: As a developer, I want e2e and UI tests to verify the notification system works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Server starts without errors with node server.js
E2E test: create a session with aiTool='claude', send simulated PTY output containing '[Y/n]', verify notification control message is broadcast within 2 seconds
E2E test: verify debounce — send pattern twice within 5 seconds, only one notification broadcast
E2E test: verify no notification for non-AI session outputting same pattern
UI test: bell icon is rendered in toolbar when page loads
UI test: clicking bell opens dropdown panel, clicking again closes it
UI test: when notification exists, clicking it calls switchSession for different session
UI test: badge count displays correctly and hides when all read
UI test: sound toggle changes localStorage value and icon state
Example: full flow — AI session triggers pattern -> server detects -> broadcasts -> client shows badge -> click bell -> see notification -> click notification -> switch to session
Negative case: server restart clears all notifications (no persistence expected)
